content_en:
And so he is left alone in his distress with no one to console him. Indeed, the weeping of Adam (‘a/ayhi as-salam) for his exile from Paradise is incomparable to the weeping of any other. His overwhelming deep sorrow and distress was not because of losing Paradise itself, but rather because of his being distanced from the Lord of Paradise.

Wahb ibn Munabbih’, may Allah be pleased with him, related: [After Adam departed from Paradise and was sent down to earth] he prostrated for one hundred years crying on the mountain of

"He refers to the ayah, “And Adam received from his Lord words so He forgave him” al-Bagarah 2: 37

? Wahb ibn Munabbih from the generation of ‘Tabi‘in. He was born during the

caliphate time of ‘Uthman bin ‘Affin (34 A.H.) He was known of his piety and honour and was an exegete in the field of Jewish narrations and tales.

Prophet Adam (‘alayhi as-salam)

India until his tears flowed into the valley of Sarandib, causing the carnation and cinnamon to gtow and caused the peacocks to migrate to the valley. Thereafter, Angel Jibra’ll came down and said to him, ’raise your head for you have been forgiven.’ So Adam (‘alaybi as-salam) taised his head and went to circulate around the Sacred House (Ka’bah) for a whole week, and scarcely had he completed before he was drowning in his tears.

’ Reported by Muslim #2751.

The Description of the Spring Season

HEN THE EARTH IS BEREFT of its partner (ie. \ x / rain), it finds itself afflicted by a touch of aridity - upon which the earth clothes itself with the garments of MSS “and you see the earth barren” [al-Hajj 22: 5]

- until it reaches a degree of severe poverty, whence it raises its hand, begging for charity from the clouds. The High Creator afterwards waters the nether from the above; aid is called forth and tain is elicited without stimulation;

“We drive them to a dead land”

Indeed the effect of the Creator on the drab and lifeless is a

wonder. Therewith, weather garbs itself in the darkest vesture while the cavalry of tain approaches, unsheathing swords of light-

The Description of the Spring Season

ning, A vagarious stratum of air begins spraying out its troops of breezes, disclosing to the wind the intention of tain. Therefore the clouds borrow the [tearful] eyelids of lovers, and the hands of the munificent, causing the valleys to abound with flowing rivers. Every time the palm of the breeze grazes these rivers, its necklace rubs against its silver chains, all the while the sun hides out, as the clouds spray their raindrops; all the while, the two partners; earth and rain, ratify a contract of mutual love.

As the winter sun begins to lay down on the immature land, the saplings begin to emerge; spring rushes into the lands lending the earth youthful garments to wear. Thereupon, the eyes of earth open after a year of sleeping, and the plants arise as if brides are adorned with every kind of jewelry; the narcissus appeats as if it were an eye and its leaves as if they were silver; the anemone shows the color of shyness, and spice plants appear as if in a state of trepidation; the viola flower gives the appearance of bite marks on the flesh; the lily nods off and then rouses; the branches of trees intertwine and then separate; and fragrances reveal their secrets to the breeze. All aesthetic songs converge in the procession of this wedding of communion; each art surpassing all the other arts. Birds competes each other in assonance; each bird expresses its yearning in its own language; the dove chants, the bulbul sermonizes, the black-headed pigeon recites, the aquatic birds whistle; the Hoopoe sings and the tree-branches sway - all of them expressing their gratitude and thankfulness to the One in Whose Hand lies all Authority. Only then does the nose of the yearner manage to detect the scent of the object of his affections.

Salman al-Farsi (radiyAllabu ‘anhu)

OREGONE DESTINY RULED in favour of a group of people with the aah,

ceded from Us - they are from it far removed”

And ruled against another group of people with

OAS GALELES “Our Lord, our wretchedness overcame us, and we were a people astray”

How woeful it is! There is no escape! The Divine providence expressed in the first ayah enlightened the hearts of the Jinn there-

Salman al-Farsi (radiyAllabu ‘anhu)

fore they said, OE Grtsy,

“Indeed, we have heard an amazing Qur’an” [al-finn 72: 1,

while the summation of disgrace pronounced in the second ayah;

“our wretchedness overcame us” [a-Mu’miniin 23: 106),

Blinded the hearts of Quraysh causing them to say [about the Quran],

OWN Vs Sy “This is not but legends of the former peoples.” fal-Anfal 8: 31)

{On the Day of Judgment] those who shall receive their books in left hand will be expelled to the distant darkness, veiled [from Allah], while those who shall receive their records in their right hand will bask in the gusts of nearness [to their Lord]. Indeed, neither did the worship of Shay/dn and Bal‘am' bring forth any benefit, nor the stubbornness of sorcerers bring forth any harm. A [Christian] priest accepted the Oneness of Allah although he never saw the Messenger (8), while on the other hand, Ibn Ubayy

' Balam ibn Ba’ira’ one of the known most knowledgeable people of Bani Israel (the Jews) who learnt the Torah directly from Misa. However, he eventually rebelled and went astray and authored a book promoting disbelief, therefore Allah revealed the following ayah about him; “And recite to them, [O Mubammad), the news of him to whors we gave [knowledge of] Our signs, but be detached himself from them; so Shaytin pursued hint, and he became of the deviators.” [al-Araf

SEEDS OF ADMONISHMENT AND REFORM

disbelieved despite the fact that he prayed behind the Messenger (). The example of the former is like the lizard [lives on waterless dry earth] but still finds sufficient water to drink and survive, whereas the example of the latter is like the fish lives in water but does not drink. The Messenger of Allah (38) visited the house of a Jew who was ill, and said to him, embrace Islam and you shall be saved. The sick child looked at his father [seeking his instruction] so the father said to his son, obey Abii al-Qasim (gg).? Thereupon, the Jewish child embraced Islam, and thus he became in his house a stranger as was Salman [al-Farsi] (redéyAllahu ‘anbnl) when he was in his house [prior to his Islam]. Nevertheless [the Prophet (@) eased the state of such foreignness with] “Salman is from

The tongues of the opponents [i.c. the Jews] yet could not be withheld so they shouted objecting, why does Muhammad not leave us alone? The wordless reply embodied in the Prophet’s state said [in regards the Jew child who embraced Islam], ‘How can I leave you alone when you have a sick person belonging to us still at your place! I still have work to do’. Indeed, the circumstances and events may harmonise hearts. Misa (‘a/ayhi as-salam) could not stand the bitterness of being a stranger at the house of pharaoh, even though the wife of pharaoh, Asiya who was a Muslim, was a source of comfort to him during his stay, because the tights of his biological mother had precedence. So, his mouth was commanded not to accept milk from any nurse,

? One of the nicknames of the Prophet (38)

Salman al-Farsi (radiyAllahn ‘anhu)

“and We had prevented from him [all] wet nurses before”

Until his real mother nursed him under the lute song of reunion. The knowledge of Allah predestined the belief of Asiya and the Prophethood of Misa (‘alayhi as-salam), therefore the casket in which he - a child separated from his mother - was steered to the doorstep of a lonely wife who was (as if) without a husband.

As Salman’s (radiyAllahu ‘anh#) predestination secured him [his guidance], the breezes of youthfulness carried him towards teligion; his father was upon the belief of the Magians but the providential evidences guided Salman to the monastery of the Chtistians. He debated his father whose only response to his son’s atguments was to put him in chains; a lowly response practiced from ages - since the day of

“T give life and cause death” [al-Bagarah 2: 258}

[which had been the response of Ibrahim’ (‘alayhi as-salam) adversary], and

[the day at which the people of Ibrahim (‘alayhi as-salam) admitted the wrongness of their belief] but [despite all that, they re-

SEEDS OF ADMONISHMENT AND REFORM

“Burn him and support your gods - if you are to act.” [al-Anbiya’ 21: 68]

At the commencement of his journey, Salman (radiyAllahu ‘anhi) became a guest to Kas Arye

“We shall wry test you” [a/-Bagarah 2: 155]

because without enduring the trial patiently he would have not reached the rank of “a man who has journeyed far, is disheveled and dusty if he swears, Allah will fulfill it for him." When he heard of some people preparing to travel, he stealthily left his father’s wary guard, and without hesitance, joined the travelers, hoping to reach the destination he sought. He dived into the ocean of resurrection to find the core of existence; his innate self raised objection [to this plan] saying, where are you going? and he replied:

BenesiSeas sus “T will go to [where I am ordered by] my Lord; He will

guide me.” [al-Saffat 37: 99]

With a humble demeanor, he devoted himself to serving the guides of his journey. Eventually, the time came when the (Chtistian) hermits realised that their era was drawing to an end, so they
content_id:
Maka ia pun ditinggalkan sendirian dalam kesedihannya tanpa seorang pun yang menghiburnya. Sungguh, tangisan Adam ('alayhi as-salam) atas pengasingannya dari Surga tidak dapat dibandingkan dengan tangisan siapa pun. Kesedihan dan kepedihan yang sangat mendalam itu bukan karena kehilangan Surga itu sendiri, melainkan karena jauhnya ia dari Rabb pemilik Surga.

Wahb ibn Munabbih, semoga Allah meridhainya, meriwayatkan: [Setelah Adam meninggalkan Surga dan diturunkan ke bumi] ia bersujud selama seratus tahun menangis di atas gunung

"Ia merujuk pada ayat, "Kemudian Adam menerima beberapa kalimat dari Tuhannya, maka Allah menerima taubatnya" al-Baqarah 2: 37

Wahb ibn Munabbih dari generasi Tabi'in. Ia lahir pada masa

kekhalifahan 'Utsman bin 'Affan (34 H.) Ia dikenal karena ketakwaan dan kemuliaannya serta merupakan seorang ahli tafsir dalam bidang riwayat dan kisah-kisah Yahudi.

Nabi Adam ('alayhi as-salam)

India hingga air matanya mengalir ke lembah Sarandib, menyebabkan bunga anyelir dan kayu manis tumbuh serta menyebabkan burung merak bermigrasi ke lembah tersebut. Setelah itu, Malaikat Jibra'il turun dan berkata kepadanya, 'angkatlah kepalamu karena engkau telah diampuni.' Maka Adam ('alayhi as-salam) mengangkat kepalanya dan pergi mengelilingi Baitullah (Ka'bah) selama satu minggu penuh, dan belum sempat ia menyelesaikannya ia sudah tenggelam dalam air matanya.

Diriwayatkan oleh Muslim #2751.

Gambaran Musim Semi

KETIKA BUMI KEHILANGAN pasangannya (yakni hujan), ia mendapati dirinya tertimpa sentuhan kekeringan - di mana bumi pun mengenakan pakaian "dan kamu lihat bumi kering tandus" [al-Hajj 22: 5]

- hingga mencapai tingkat kemiskinan yang parah, lalu ia mengangkat tangannya, meminta sedekah dari awan. Sang Pencipta Yang Maha Tinggi kemudian menyirami yang di bawah dari atas; pertolongan didatangkan dan hujan diturunkan tanpa rangsangan;

"Kami halau mereka ke suatu negeri yang mati"

Sungguh pengaruh Sang Pencipta terhadap yang suram dan tak bernyawa adalah suatu

keajaiban. Dengan itu, cuaca mengenakan jubah yang paling gelap sementara pasukan hujan mendekat, menghunus pedang-pedang kilat.

Gambaran Musim Semi

Lapisan udara yang berubah-ubah mulai menyebarkan pasukan angin sepoi-sepoi, mengungkapkan kepada angin niat sang hujan. Maka awan pun meminjam kelopak mata para pecinta yang penuh air mata, dan tangan para dermawan, menyebabkan lembah-lembah dipenuhi sungai yang mengalir. Setiap kali telapak tangan angin sepoi menyentuh sungai-sungai ini, kalungnya bergesek dengan rantai peraknya, sementara matahari bersembunyi, saat awan menyemprotkan tetes-tetes hujannya; sementara itu, kedua pasangan; bumi dan hujan, mengesahkan perjanjian cinta timbal balik.

Saat matahari musim dingin mulai menyinari tanah yang masih muda, tunas-tunas mulai bermunculan; musim semi bergegas memasuki daratan meminjamkan bumi pakaian muda untuk dikenakan. Maka mata bumi terbuka setelah setahun tertidur, dan tumbuh-tumbuhan bangkit bagai pengantin yang dihias dengan segala macam perhiasan; bunga narsis tampak seolah ia adalah mata dan daun-daunnya seolah perak; bunga anemon menampakkan warna malu, dan rempah-rempah tampak seolah dalam keadaan gentar; bunga viola memberikan penampilan seperti bekas gigitan pada kulit; bunga bakung terlelap lalu terbangun; dahan-dahan pohon saling terjalin lalu berpisah; dan wewangian menyingkap rahasia mereka kepada angin sepoi. Seluruh nyanyian keindahan berkumpul dalam arak-arakan pernikahan persatuan ini; setiap seni melampaui seni yang lainnya. Burung-burung saling berlomba dalam keselarasan; setiap burung mengungkapkan kerinduannya dalam bahasanya sendiri; merpati bernyanyi, bulbul berkhotbah, merpati berkepala hitam membaca, burung air bersiul; burung Hud-hud bernyanyi dan dahan-dahan pohon bergoyang - semuanya mengungkapkan rasa syukur dan terima kasih mereka kepada Yang di Tangan-Nya segala Kekuasaan. Barulah saat itu hidung orang yang merindukan berhasil menangkap aroma dari yang dirindukannya.

Salman al-Farsi (radiyAllahu 'anhu)

TAKDIR YANG TELAH DITETAPKAN memutuskan untuk sekelompok orang dengan

"Sesungguhnya orang-orang yang telah ada untuk mereka kebaikan [balasan] dari Kami - mereka dari (neraka) itu dijauhkan"

Dan memutuskan terhadap kelompok lain dengan

"Ya Tuhan kami, kesengsaraan kami telah menguasai kami, dan kami adalah kaum yang sesat"

Betapa menyedihkannya! Tidak ada jalan keluar! Takdir Ilahi yang tertuang dalam ayat pertama menerangi hati-hati para Jin sehingga

Salman al-Farsi (radiyAllahu 'anhu)

mereka berkata,

"Sesungguhnya kami telah mendengarkan al-Quran yang menakjubkan" [al-Jinn 72: 1],

sementara puncak kehinaan yang terucap dalam ayat kedua;

"kesengsaraan kami telah menguasai kami" [al-Mu'minun 23: 106],

Membutakan hati-hati Quraysy sehingga mereka berkata [tentang al-Quran],

"Ini tidak lain hanyalah dongeng-dongeng orang terdahulu." [al-Anfal 8: 31]

[Pada Hari Kiamat] mereka yang menerima kitab mereka di tangan kiri akan diusir ke kegelapan yang jauh, terhalang [dari Allah], sementara mereka yang menerima catatan mereka di tangan kanan akan bersuka cita dalam hembusan kedekatan [kepada Tuhan mereka]. Sungguh, ibadahnya Syaithan dan Bal'am tidak mendatangkan manfaat apa pun, dan kekerasan kepala para tukang sihir pun tidak mendatangkan mudarat apa pun. Seorang pendeta [Kristen] menerima keesaan Allah meskipun ia tidak pernah melihat Rasulullah (ﷺ), sementara di sisi lain, Ibnu Ubayy

Bal'am ibn Ba'ira' adalah salah satu orang yang paling berilmu di kalangan Bani Israel (Yahudi) yang mempelajari Taurat langsung dari Musa. Namun, ia akhirnya memberontak dan tersesat serta menulis sebuah kitab yang mempromosikan kekufuran, maka Allah menurunkan ayat berikut tentangnya; "Dan bacakanlah kepada mereka, [wahai Muhammad], berita tentang orang yang telah Kami berikan kepadanya ayat-ayat Kami, kemudian dia melepaskan diri darinya; lalu dia diikuti oleh Syaithan, dan dia termasuk orang-orang yang sesat." [al-A'raf

BENIH-BENIH NASIHAT DAN PERBAIKAN

kafir meskipun ia salat di belakang Rasulullah (ﷺ). Perumpamaan yang pertama seperti kadal [yang hidup di tanah kering tanpa air] namun tetap menemukan air yang cukup untuk diminum dan bertahan hidup, sedangkan perumpamaan yang kedua seperti ikan yang hidup di air namun tidak minum. Rasulullah (ﷺ) mengunjungi rumah seorang Yahudi yang sedang sakit, dan berkata kepadanya, masuklah Islam niscaya engkau akan selamat. Anak yang sakit itu memandang ayahnya [mencari petunjuknya] maka sang ayah berkata kepada anaknya, taatilah Abu al-Qasim (ﷺ). Maka anak Yahudi itu pun memeluk Islam, dan dengan demikian ia menjadi orang asing di rumahnya sebagaimana Salman [al-Farsi] (radiyAllahu 'anhu) ketika ia berada di rumahnya [sebelum Islamnya]. Meskipun demikian [Nabi (ﷺ) meringankan keadaan keterasingan tersebut dengan] "Salman adalah dari

Lidah-lidah para penentang [yakni orang-orang Yahudi] tetap tidak dapat ditahan sehingga mereka berteriak keberatan, mengapa Muhammad tidak membiarkan kami? Jawaban tanpa kata yang terwujud dalam keadaan Nabi berkata [mengenai anak Yahudi yang memeluk Islam], 'Bagaimana aku bisa membiarkan kalian sementara kalian masih memiliki orang sakit milik kami di tempat kalian! Aku masih memiliki pekerjaan yang harus dilakukan.' Sungguh, keadaan dan peristiwa dapat menyelaraskan hati-hati. Musa ('alayhi as-salam) tidak sanggup menanggung pahitnya menjadi orang asing di rumah Fir'aun, meskipun istri Fir'aun, Asiya yang seorang Muslimah, menjadi sumber penghiburan baginya selama tinggal di sana, karena hak ibu kandungnya lebih didahulukan. Maka mulutnya diperintahkan untuk tidak menerima susu dari pengasuh mana pun,

Salah satu julukan Nabi (ﷺ)

Salman al-Farsi (radiyAllahu 'anhu)

"dan Kami telah mengharamkan kepadanya [semua] ibu susuan sebelumnya"

Hingga ibu kandungnya menyusuinya di bawah alunan lagu perjumpaan kembali. Ilmu Allah telah mentakdirkan keimanan Asiya dan kenabian Musa ('alayhi as-salam), maka peti tempat ia - seorang anak yang dipisahkan dari ibunya - diarahkan ke ambang pintu seorang istri yang kesepian yang (seolah) tanpa suami.

Sebagaimana takdir Salman (radiyAllahu 'anhu) menjaminnya [hidayahnya], hembusan masa muda membawanya menuju agama; ayahnya memeluk kepercayaan Majusi namun bukti-bukti takdir Ilahi membimbing Salman ke biara orang-orang Kristen. Ia berdebat dengan ayahnya yang satu-satunya respons terhadap argumen putranya adalah membelenggunya dengan rantai; respons yang rendah yang telah dipraktikkan sejak dahulu kala - sejak hari

"Aku yang menghidupkan dan mematikan" [al-Baqarah 2: 258]

[yang merupakan respons musuh Ibrahim ('alayhi as-salam)], dan

"Kemudian mereka berbalik arah, [berkata], 'Sungguh kamu telah mengetahui bahwa berhala-berhala ini tidak dapat berbicara!'" [al-Anbiya' 21: 65]

[hari di mana kaum Ibrahim ('alayhi as-salam) mengakui kesalahan keyakinan mereka] namun [meskipun demikian, mereka

BENIH-BENIH NASIHAT DAN PERBAIKAN

"Bakarlah dia dan tolonglah tuhan-tuhan kalian - jika kalian hendak bertindak." [al-Anbiya' 21: 68]

Di awal perjalanannya, Salman (radiyAllahu 'anhu) menjadi tamu bagi

"Sungguh Kami akan menguji kalian" [al-Baqarah 2: 155]

karena tanpa menanggung ujian dengan sabar ia tidak akan mencapai derajat "seorang yang telah menempuh perjalanan jauh, rambutnya kusut dan berdebu, jika ia bersumpah atas nama Allah, niscaya Allah akan memenuhinya." Ketika ia mendengar tentang sekelompok orang yang bersiap untuk bepergian, ia diam-diam meninggalkan penjagaan ketat ayahnya, dan tanpa ragu-ragu, bergabung dengan para musafir, berharap mencapai tujuan yang ia cari. Ia menyelam ke dalam lautan kebangkitan untuk menemukan inti keberadaan; diri batinnya mengajukan keberatan [atas rencana ini] berkata, hendak ke mana engkau? dan ia menjawab:

"Aku akan pergi ke [tempat yang diperintahkan oleh] Tuhanku; Dia akan membimbing-ku." [al-Saffat 37: 99]

Dengan sikap yang rendah hati, ia mengabdikan dirinya untuk melayani para pemandu perjalanannya. Akhirnya, tiba saatnya ketika para pertapa (Kristen) menyadari bahwa masa mereka hampir berakhir, maka mereka
